title: Node Status Topic Payload
description: JSON Schema for payloads published by nodes to hexe/nodes/<node_id>/status. Node status topics should be published as retained last-known health snapshots. Core should evaluate freshness from reported_at, not rely on MQTT message expiry as the primary source of truth for node liveness. Current recommended thresholds are under 5 minutes healthy or fresh, 5 to 30 minutes stale or degraded, and over 30 minutes inactive or offline.
$comment: Recommended behavior: retain the latest status snapshot; mark status stale when now - reported_at exceeds the freshness threshold; mark the node inactive or offline when now - reported_at exceeds the inactivity threshold. MQTT message expiry may be used as secondary cleanup, but its expiry window should be longer than the Core inactivity threshold so diagnostic last-known state is preserved. The current recommended thresholds are stale after 300 seconds and inactive after 1800 seconds.

node_id.description: Canonical node identifier. This must match the <node_id> topic segment.

health_status.description: Current health classification for the node.

reported_at.description: UTC timestamp when the node generated this health payload.

ttl_s.description: How long Core may consider this status fresh if no newer payload arrives. Keep this aligned with the current freshness policy of fresh under 5 minutes, stale from 5 to 30 minutes, and inactive over 30 minutes unless Core is configured otherwise.

lifecycle_state.description: Optional node lifecycle state such as capability_setup_pending, operational, or degraded.

ready.description: Whether the node currently considers itself available for work.

checks.description: Optional named health checks and their values.
